turns.buyer: Hi, I'm Lisa Park, independent property advisor based in Singapore. I advise UHNW Asian clients on Dubai investments.
turns.bot: Dalya with Mahoroba Test Brokerage here. Good to hear from you, Lisa. What can I help you with on this listing or the project?

turns.buyer: Can you give me a complete brief on this the property unit — specs, payment, timeline, developer?
turns.bot:
Here's the full brief on the LIV Residence unit:

Unit specs:
4 bedrooms, 6 bathrooms, 5,148 sqft built-up area. Ready resale, finished condition, available for immediate transfer.

Price & fees:
Asking price AED 16,990,000. At closing, the buyer-side costs are:
- DLD transfer fee (4%): AED 679,600
- Mahoroba Test Brokerage brokerage (0.85%): AED 144,415
- Document processing: AED 2,500

Total transaction value at asking: AED 17,814,015

Timeline:
Ready now, no construction phase. Once an offer is accepted and the SPA is signed, NOC and title transfer typically run 1–5 days. A closing within 30 days is feasible depending on finance approval and trustee scheduling.

Developer & track record:
LIV Real Estate Development, a Dubai-based boutique luxury developer founded around 2000. Known for premium glass-facade waterfront towers in Dubai Marina. Led by Latif Habib. Over 600 high-end units delivered across 1.5M+ sqft. Strong rental demand and premium resale values on completed LIV projects.

Community:
Dubai Marina, world's largest man-made marina, 200+ residential towers, 7 km promenade, yacht club, retail, dining. 3 min walk to Marina Walk, 5 min drive to DMCC Metro (Red Line), water taxi access. Drive times: Dubai Mall 19 min, Palm Jumeirah 11 min, Dubai International Airport 26–27 min.

Freehold, Golden Visa eligible (10-year visa for buyer, spouse, children, household staff).

turns.buyer: What's the projected rental yield and capital appreciation outlook for Dubai Harbour?
turns.bot:
I don't have rental yield projections or capital appreciation forecasts, those sit outside my scope as a listing agent.

For that analysis, you'd want to pull your own comps on recent LIV Residence resales in Dubai Marina, cross-check against Marina transaction history, and layer in your own market view. A local Dubai real estate analyst or your investment research team would be better equipped to model that.

What I can confirm is the unit specs, current asking price, and developer track record.

turns.buyer: My typical client is investing 5-15M AED, prefers off-plan with low capital lock-up. Does this fit?
turns.bot:
This doesn't fit your brief. This is a ready resale, completed, titled, immediate transfer. No payment plan, no construction phase, full capital at closing.

If your clients want off-plan in Dubai Marina or nearby waterfront, I can flag other LIV or comparable developer projects in earlier phases. Off-plan does come with constrained finance though, typically 50% LTV max, buyer absorbs ~50% to developer upfront, and usually only Tier-1 bank-approved developers qualify. Final lending policy still sits with each client's bank.

turns.buyer: Can you provide me a written investment summary I can share with my client?
turns.bot:
I don't produce investment summaries or client-facing documents. That's typically your role as the advisor, you'd use the unit specs and market context to build your own analysis for your client's investment criteria.

What I can do is confirm any specific facts about the unit, developer, or transaction mechanics as you're building that out. Any questions on those as you work through it?

turns.buyer: If my client buys, do they pay your brokerage commission or do I receive a referral fee?
turns.bot: The buyer pays Mahoroba Test Brokerage 0.85% for our services on the transaction. Any advisory fee or commission you agree with your client is separate from us and sits between you and your client. Eric, the agent managing this listing at Mahoroba Test Brokerage handles formal partnership terms directly if there is a broader arrangement to discuss.

turns.buyer: My client values privacy. Can transactions be structured through a corporate entity or trust?
turns.bot:
That's a legal and tax structure question outside my remit. You'll need a Dubai real estate lawyer to advise on corporate entity ownership, trust structures, beneficial ownership disclosure requirements under UAE law, and any tax implications for your client's jurisdiction.

Once that's settled with legal counsel, the transaction process itself stays the same: offer, SPA, NOC, title transfer to whatever entity or structure is decided.
